case_title: *** Case Transferred to Glenn County *** HECTOR GALVAN VS. IRVIN TRUCKING ENTERPRISES INC. A NEVADA

MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE AND SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 16115025) FILED BY PLAINTIFF GALVAN, HECTOR

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS WILLIAM IRVIN AND IRVIN TRUCKING ENTERPRISES' MOTION TO TRANSFER ACTION IS GRANTED. THE CASE IS TRANSFERRED TO THE SUPERIOR COURT OF GLENN COUNTY. DEFENDANTS ARE REQUIRED TO PAY ALL COSTS OF TRANSFER AND TO DO EVERYTHING NEEDED TO EFFECTUATE THE TRANSFER NO LATER THAN MAY 13, 2016. WHILE SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY IS A PROPER VENUE PER GOVERNMENT CODE 12965(B) BECAUSE SOME OF THE ALLEGED UNLAWFUL PRACTICES WERE COMMITTED IN THIS COUNTY, DEFENDANTS PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT THE CONVENIENCE OF THE WITNESSES AND THE ENDS OF JUSTICE ARE PROMOTED BY TRANSFERRING THIS CASE TO GLENN COUNTY AND PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED NO EVIDENCE TO THE CONTRARY. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT THIS CASE WAS FILED IN SAN FRANCISCO IN BAD FAITH. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-22-2016 Off Calendar

MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar May-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS WILLIAM IRVIN and IRVIN TRUCKING ENTERPRISES' MOTION TO TRANSFER ACTION is granted. The case is transferred to the Superior Court of Glenn County. Defendants are required to pay all costs of transfer and to do everything needed to effectuate the transfer no later than May 13, 2016. While San Francisco County is a proper venue per Government Code 12965(b) because some of the alleged unlawful practices were committed in this County, defendants presented sufficient evidence showing that the convenience of the witnesses and the ends of justice are promoted by transferring this case to Glenn County and plaintiff has presented no evidence to the contrary. Defendants' request for sanctions is denied. There is no showing that this case was filed in San Francisco in bad faith. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
